behavior_summary: U8P_MELSDOOR::leaveFastArea: When the actor/item leaves the fast-area trigger, the script reads the egg's numeric ID via Egg::getEggId(this). If the ID is >= 100, it subtracts 100 and writes the result back with Egg::setEggId(this, newId). This effectively decrements or wraps an egg-related counter by 100 on area exit.

U8P_MELSDOOR::hatch: Spawns/creates another object, invokes DOOR_NS::useDoor on the spawned object (script suspends until that completes), reads the spawned object's quality bitfield via Item::getQuality, clears bit 0x0400 from that bitfield and writes it back via Item::setQuality, then destroys the original egg item with Item::destroy. This sequence appears to finalize a 'U8P_MELSDOOR::hatch' and remove an 'unhatched' flag from the created object.
notable_control_flow: Conditional branch in U8P_MELSDOOR::leaveFastArea: if Egg::getEggId(this) >= 0x64 then Egg::setEggId(this, Egg::getEggId(this) - 0x64).
U8P_MELSDOOR::hatch performs a spawn + DOOR_NS::useDoor call and suspends until completion, then performs quality-bit manipulation (clear 0x0400) and destroys the original egg item.
open_questions: What exact object/type is spawned by U8P_MELSDOOR::hatch (spawn parameters are not present in the excerpt)?
What is the semantic meaning of bit 0x0400 in the quality bitfield (inferred as an 'unhatched' flag but not confirmed)?
Under which explicit game events/processes is U8P_MELSDOOR::hatch invoked in normal gameplay (the file shows it exists as a process but not where it is triggered)?
Does subtracting 100 from the egg ID represent an index wrap, a stage decrement, or a multi-egg count mechanic? (inferred but not explicit)

main_function.name: U8P_MELSDOOR::leaveFastArea
main_function.purpose: On leaving the fast-area trigger, ensure the egg's internal numeric ID is wrapped/decremented by 100 if it is 100 or greater. This maintains or normalizes the egg's internal counter/state when the actor exits the region.
entry_conditions: Triggered by the fast-area 'leave' event (actor/item exits the trigger region).
Implicit parameter: 'this' refers to the egg item instance being processed.

function_map.name: U8P_MELSDOOR::leaveFastArea
function_map.role: area-exit guard that normalizes/wraps an egg ID by subtracting 100 when >=100

function_map.name: U8P_MELSDOOR::hatch
function_map.role: spawn-and-finalize helper that creates the hatched entity, clears a specific quality bit (0x0400) on it, and destroys the source egg

function_map.name: spawn
function_map.role: instantiate/create another object and return a reference

function_map.name: DOOR_NS::useDoor
function_map.role: run the spawned object's activation/door-use behavior (called and awaited during U8P_MELSDOOR::hatch)

external_calls.purpose: Read the egg's numeric ID to decide whether to subtract 100.
Write the decremented/wrapped egg ID (original - 100) back to the egg instance if threshold met.
Create/instantiate the target object that represents the hatched entity; returned reference stored to a local.
Invoke the spawned object's activation/door logic; the script suspends until this completes, part of the U8P_MELSDOOR::hatch flow.
Read the spawned item's quality/flags so a specific bit (0x0400) can be cleared.
Write the modified quality back after clearing bit 0x0400.
Destroy the original egg item after U8P_MELSDOOR::hatch finalization.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: U8P_MELSDOOR::leaveFastArea: When the actor/item leaves the fast-area trigger, the script reads the egg's numeric ID via Egg::getEggId(this). If the ID is >= 100, it subtracts 100 and writes the result back with Egg::setEggId(this, newId). This effectively decrements or wraps an egg-related counter by 100 on area exit.
